title: fix can't run by source code
body:
the error is:    fix NoMatches: No nodes match <class 'textual.widgets._rich_log.RichLog'> on XHSDownloader

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fixes a Textual startup crash (`NoMatches` for `RichLog`) when running from source by making RichLog lookup lifecycle-safe and deterministic. The plan guards/defers the startup query, adds a stable RichLog selector on the Index screen, and ensures settings validation logs only after the Index widget tree is mounted.

steps.operation: Make RichLog lookup at startup resilient (guard query / defer validation)
steps.reason: `self.screen.query_one(RichLog)` can run before the Index screen’s widget tree is fully mounted/active, raising Textual `NoMatches` and crashing the app when running from source. Wrap the lookup in `try/except` (catching Textual’s `NoMatches`) and/or defer the settings check until after the screen is mounted; pass `None` (or a safe fallback logger target) into the settings callback when RichLog isn’t available.
steps.side_effects: Startup flow changes slightly (settings validation/log message may occur a tick later). If other code relied on immediate settings validation, it may now happen after the screen push/mount. Add/adjust tests/manual checks for first-run (missing settings keys) and confirm no crash + message still appears (RichLog when available, console fallback otherwise).

steps.operation: Assign a stable selector to the Index RichLog to enable deterministic querying
steps.reason: Type-based querying (`query_one(RichLog)`) is more brittle during early lifecycle and can collide with multiple RichLog instances across screens. Giving the RichLog an explicit `id` (e.g., `id="app_log"`) allows `query_one("#app_log")` from the correct screen and reduces timing/ambiguity issues.
steps.side_effects: Any CSS, bindings, or code that assumes the previous structure may need updating if it starts using the new `id`. If additional screens also define RichLog, ensure IDs remain unique per screen context (or query within the Index screen only).

steps.operation: Ensure settings validation/log wiring happens when the Index screen is mounted and RichLog is guaranteed to exist
steps.reason: Moving (or triggering) the settings check from within `Index.on_mount` ensures `self.query_one(RichLog)` succeeds because the screen has been mounted and its widget tree is present. This avoids the startup race between `push_screen()` and querying the widget tree from the app’s `on_mount`. If the app must own validation, `Index.on_mount` can instead expose the RichLog reference or call back into the app after mount.
steps.side_effects: Behavior changes in when/where missing-settings messages are emitted (now tied to Index mounting). If other screens are pushed first in some modes, validation might be delayed until Index appears unless explicitly handled. Verify monitor/other screens still function and that the log destination is correct after navigation.
